description: Test cases for iOS date and time picker interactions

evals.prompt: Select December 25, 2026 from a UIDatePicker in an iOS app.
evals.expected_output:
⚠️ NOT TESTED YET
Expected behavior:
1. Call ui.inspect to find UIDatePicker path
2. Call ui.datePicker.setDate with date='2026-12-25'
3. Picker should visually update to selected date
4. Verify selection with ui.inspect (if picker exposes selected value)
5. Requires testing before production use

evals.prompt: Select 14:30 (2:30 PM) from a time picker in an iOS app.
evals.expected_output:
⚠️ NOT TESTED YET
Expected behavior:
1. Find UIDatePicker in time mode
2. Call ui.datePicker.setTime with time='14:30'
3. Picker wheels should scroll to 2:30 PM
4. Verify time selection
5. Requires testing before production use

evals.prompt: Use swipe gestures as a workaround to manually scroll a date picker wheel.
evals.expected_output:
Workaround approach:
1. Call ui.inspect to find UIPickerView or UIDatePicker elementRef
2. Call ui.swipe with withinElementRef=<picker_ref>, direction='up', distance=0.3
3. Swipe up scrolls picker down (selects lower/earlier values)
4. Swipe down scrolls picker up (selects higher/later values)
5. Multiple swipes may be needed to reach target value
6. No precise value selection - trial and error
7. ⚠️ Not recommended for production - unreliable
